bead.title: ddx agent route-status: consume upstream RouteDecision.Candidates + typed no-viable-candidate error
bead.description:
## Context

Follow-up to ddx-fdd3ea36 (D6, dropped from that bead's reduced scope). Upstream work (agent-53f38d95) is **satisfied**: `agent.RouteDecision.Candidates []RouteCandidate` and the typed no-viable-candidate error (recoverable via `errors.As`) are now public in the routing point release.

This bead tracks the DDx-side consumer work that hooks the new decision trace into operator-facing surfaces.

## Scope

1. **`ddx agent route-status` CLI.** Render per-profile decision trace. For each profile (default, local, standard, ...), call `agentlib.ResolveRoute` and print the full `Candidates` slice: harness, provider, endpoint, model, score, eligible, reason. On no-viable-candidate, render the rejection heatmap from the typed error.

2. **Providers UI decision-trace.** Hooks into ddx-23978824 (providers UI). For each harness/provider row, surface the most recent rejection reason from `RouteDecision.Candidates`. Per-harness per-provider rejection heatmap as a secondary view.

3. **Bead `kind:routing` evidence events.** Include the full `Candidates` trace in evidence records, not just the winning pick. Ties into ddx-fdd3ea36's D3 preflight gate so failed-route-before-claim records carry the trace.

## Upstream dependency

agent-53f38d95 — **satisfied**. Public types and typed error are tagged in the routing point release that ddx-fdd3ea36 pulls.

## Independence note

ddx-05b4cc9d (`workersByProject` id/path filter) is independent of this work and **not blocked by this bead**. It is a separate GraphQL filter bug; this bead does not need to wait on it nor does it block it.
bead.acceptance:
1. `ddx agent route-status` command exists and renders per-profile decision trace consuming `RouteDecision.Candidates`.
2. On no-viable-candidate, command extracts the typed error via `errors.As` and renders rejection reasons per candidate.
3. Providers UI (ddx-23978824 surface) renders rejection-reason column / heatmap from `RouteDecision.Candidates`.
4. Routing evidence events include full `Candidates` slice, not just winning pick.
5. Tests cover: success path trace, no-viable-candidate trace, rejection-reason rendering.
6. Upstream dep agent-53f38d95 is referenced in this bead and confirmed satisfied at implementation time.
